artPrompt: A weary human bureaucrat in drab municipal uniform plants himself like an immovable bollard behind a velvet rope at a service window, arms crossed, a blank illegible number dispenser glowing faintly beside him. Behind, an endless beige queue snakes back into fluorescent gloom. Bold readable silhouette dominates the lower frame while the corridor recedes upward. Wide 4:3 landscape, cold cyan glow, oppressive stillness, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: DMV Line Warden
text:
Defender, vigilance
Whenever a creature attacks you, its controller takes a number. That creature can't attack you again until they are called, which is never.
flavorText: "Now serving number 4. We are currently on number 4. We will be on number 4 for some time."
